greeting: Morning, Haniel. Clean calendar, fresh task from yesterday's Mayara meeting — let's get after it.
hype: New client feature, three RBAC wins, a quick modal kill, and QB work that clears the path to production. No meetings, no blockers. Stack the wins and make this day count.
notice: Before the afternoon CT Pros block: drop your Intuit sandbox credentials into .env — t29 needs them or it stalls.

tasks.title: "OK to draft" override — start design without final jobsite measurements (case-by-case)
tasks.notes: FEATURE A from 2026-06-24 Mayara alignment. After deposit, project goes to designer. Case-by-case: if site is known (repeat client), Mayara OKs drafting without measurements.

prep.what: Add a case-by-case override so Mayara can unlock design-phase start before jobsite measurements are recorded — for repeat clients with known sites.
prep.understand: Right now the phase-transition logic gates design start on measurements being complete. The override is NOT automatic — it's Mayara's explicit approval per project. You need to wire a gated action into the project hub, an `ok_to_draft` flag on the `projects` table, and an OR-condition in the existing measurements gate. Came straight out of yesterday's client meeting — it's a real workflow gap.
approach: Find the exact gate in `RecomputeProjectPhase` or `transition()` that blocks design start when measurements are missing — that's your insertion point
Add `ok_to_draft` boolean to `projects` table (migration + fillable, default false)
Add an 'OK to draft' action on the project hub visible to owner/project_manager — controller action sets the flag, logs to ActivityLog
Update the design-start gate: `measurements_complete OR ok_to_draft = true` — add a feature test covering both branches
resources: `RecomputeProjectPhase` service or `transition()` method (find the measurements gate)
Project hub Vue component (where to add the button)
Permission gate for who can set `ok_to_draft` (owner / project_manager)

tasks.title: Ungated-route audit — zero-perm user must not 200 any gated page
tasks.notes: Cheapest high-value net: user with NO permissions must not receive 200 on any non-public route. Maintain allowlist of intentionally-public routes.

prep.what: Fire every registered route as a zero-permission authenticated user and assert none return 200 — catches any endpoint where `CheckPermission` middleware was accidentally skipped.
prep.understand: `CheckPermission` is the enforcement layer. Any route without it will let a user with no permissions through. The test is simple: seed a user with no roles/permissions, iterate every route, assert non-200 for anything outside the known public allowlist. Build the allowlist first — `/d/{share_token}`, `/login`, `/register`, `/logout`, health checks. t651 RBAC work already done — this is the automated regression to lock it in.
approach: Create (or reuse) a test user with no roles and no permissions — confirm zero grants in DB before running
Pull routes via `php artisan route:list --json`, filter to non-public (exclude the allowlist)
Write a PHPUnit feature test iterating each route: authenticate as zero-perm user, assert response status != 200
Log any 200s as findings — fix each by adding the missing `CheckPermission` middleware
resources: `php artisan route:list` (pipe to JSON for easy iteration)
`UserFactory` — how to seed a user with no roles

tasks.title: Permission-matrix anomaly report for owner review
tasks.notes: Generate role→permission matrix + flag suspicious entries. Surface to owner for sign-off. FINDINGS already done from t651.

prep.what: Format the t651 RBAC findings into a role × permission matrix and flag anything suspicious — then surface it to the owner for an explicit sign-off before locking it as a regression test in t654.
prep.understand: Discovery is done (t651). This task is formatting + presenting. The system has 3 roles and 11 permission modules. Anomalies to surface: a role with access it shouldn't have, a role missing something it needs, any dead permission no one has, or any role with blanket access. Owner sign-off is required before the matrix becomes a regression suite (t654). Rule: infer, don't ask — only surface genuinely flagged rows.
approach: Pull current role→permission assignments from `PermissionSeeder` or DB — build a 3×11 matrix (role rows × permission columns)
Cross-reference with t651 findings — flag over-privileged grants, under-privileged gaps, dead permissions
Format the output as a Markdown table or a `/dev/rbac-matrix` route the owner can view
Attach a concise summary: how many anomalies, what they are, and what the recommended fix is for each
resources: The 11 permission module names (list from the seeder)
Owner review channel / how to share the report

tasks.title: Materials catalog edit modal does NOT close after successful save (.modal-backdrop bug)
tasks.phase: Phase 78 · V1 Polishing Crusade — bug hunt + 100% E2E coverage
tasks.notes: Repro confirmed. Hypothesis: regressed from 311f2dcf terminology sweep. e2e: catalog-and-drafter-access.local.spec.ts:105.

prep.what: Fix the materials catalog edit modal: it's not closing after a successful save, leaving a lingering `.modal-backdrop` that blocks the next click.
prep.understand: Repro is confirmed. The backdrop bug is nearly always a modal's `v-model` or `show` prop not being reset to false after the API call — or a prop/emit name that was silently broken by a rename. Commit `311f2dcf` (the terminology sweep) is the prime suspect: if a prop or emit was renamed in the sweep and the parent component wasn't updated, the close signal is now a no-op.
approach: Open DevTools on `/catalog`, trigger edit on any item, save — confirm `.modal-backdrop` stays in DOM after the 200 response
Check the save handler: is it calling `this.showModal = false` (or similar) inside the success callback? Or is the close event never emitted?
Run `git show 311f2dcf -- <catalog-component>` — look for any renamed prop or emit that controls modal visibility
Fix the close trigger, then run `catalog-and-drafter-access.local.spec.ts` line 105 to verify the e2e goes green
resources: The `Modal` or `Dialog` base component (what props/emits it expects)

tasks.title: Labor rates + overhead costs (Rony, Giovanne, all roles)
tasks.notes: KB §6.4 + §6.5 placeholders. Rony's GSAFI LTDA rate, Giovanne's role + rate, monthly overhead. Required for financial dashboard (t51).

prep.what: Lock in the real labor rates and monthly overhead costs into KB §6.4–6.5 — these are live placeholders that every financial calculation in GS-ERP reads from.
prep.understand: Cost-plus pricing, margin rollups, and the bonus system all pull from these KB fields. Right now they're zeroes or placeholders — which means every financial output in GS-ERP is fictional. This was on yesterday's plan too. It needs to close today: 30 minutes, a phone call, and you're done. The financial dashboard (t51) is blocked on this.
approach: Open KB §6.4 and §6.5 — list every blank field before you pick up the phone
15-min call with Rony: confirm GSAFI LTDA contract rate, Giovanne's role and pay
Collect monthly overhead line items: rent, utilities, shop insurance, software subscriptions
Fill in KB with the real numbers — commit the update
Flag t51 (financial dashboard) that its data dependency is now cleared
resources: Rony contact (for GSAFI LTDA rate + Giovanne details)
t51 financial dashboard task (unblocks after this is done)

tasks.title: Initial mapping wizard: expense categories → QB accounts, default income account, tax codes, existing-record matching
tasks.notes: After connecting QB, walk Admin through a 4-step wizard. Persist mappings in settings table (qb_category_account_map JSON, qb_default_income_account_id, qb_tax_code_id). Accessible from Settings → QuickBooks → Edit Mapping.

prep.what: Build the first-run QuickBooks setup wizard in Settings — a 4-step flow that maps expense categories to QB accounts, picks a default income account, maps the tax code, and fuzzy-matches existing clients/subcontractors to QB Customers/Vendors.
prep.understand: OAuth (t17) and the API client (t18) are done. This wizard is the required next step — without these mappings the 6 sync services will hard-fail: `ExpenseSync` has no account to push to, `InvoiceSync` has no `TaxCode`, entity syncs will create duplicates. Steps 1–3 query live QB lists. Step 4 prevents duplicates by fuzzy-matching names and writing `qb_id` back to local records before the first real sync runs.
approach: Scaffold a 4-step wizard at a new `/settings/quickbooks/mapping` Inertia page (only reachable when QB is connected)
Step 1: call `QuickBooksClient` with `SELECT * FROM Account WHERE Active=true` — render a dropdown per `ExpenseCategory`; on save write `qb_category_account_map` JSON to settings
Steps 2 + 3: same Account query filtered to income accounts, then a `TaxCode` query — single-select dropdowns; persist `qb_default_income_account_id` and `qb_tax_code_id`

Step 4: fetch QB `Customer` + `Vendor` lists, run `similar_text()` or Levenshtein against local Clients/Subcontractors, show top matches with a confirm checkbox — on confirm write `qb_id` back to the local record
resources: QB v3 API: Account entity (`SELECT * FROM Account WHERE Active=true`)
QB v3 API: TaxCode, Customer, Vendor entities
`ExpenseCategory` model (all categories for the Step 1 dropdown)
Existing `/settings/quickbooks` page (the connect/disconnect UI to extend from)

tasks.title: A.5/R4 — dev QB inspector (/dev/qb-inspector) to simulate approve/reject/paid
tasks.notes: Port GS /dev/qb-inspector (gated on QB_FAKE). UI to simulate client-approved/rejected estimate + invoice-paid against fake_qb_* tables. Last remaining A.5 task (R1/R2/R3 done).

prep.what: Port the GS project's `/dev/qb-inspector` tool to CT Pros — a `QB_FAKE`-gated developer UI that lets you simulate Intuit events (estimate approved/rejected, invoice paid) against the `fake_qb_*` tables, so Section F's approval pipeline can be tested locally.
prep.understand: R1 (transport robustness), R2 (per-entity sync services), and R3 (fake-QB layer) are all done. R4 is the inspector that exercises the fake layer interactively. Without it you can't trigger Section F (t31 approval flow) locally — you'd need a real Intuit sandbox for every test run. GS has an identical pattern: the port should be near-mechanical.
approach: Open the GS codebase `/dev/qb-inspector` — read the route, controller, and Vue page; note what `fake_qb_*` fields it writes
Register the route in CT Pros `web.php` behind a `QB_FAKE=true` env gate (local-only by design)
Create the controller + Inertia Vue page: list `fake_qb_estimates` and `fake_qb_invoices`, add buttons for approve/reject (estimate) and paid (invoice)
Each button hits a POST endpoint that updates the `fake_qb_*` row status — smoke-test by clicking approve and confirming the row changes in DB
resources: How GS gates the route (the `QB_FAKE` env check pattern)
t31 Section F notes (to understand what fields the approval poller reads)

tasks.title: Decide Vendor1099 mapping (w9_on_file vs is_1099_eligible)
tasks.notes: VendorSync maps Vendor1099=w9_on_file, but those are different concepts. Pushing Vendor1099=false for 1099-eligible vendors without a W9 corrupts QB tax flags.

prep.what: Fix the semantic mismatch in `VendorSync`: QB's `Vendor1099` flag means 'send a 1099 at year-end', not 'we have their W9 on file' — add `is_1099_eligible` to subcontractors and use that instead.
prep.understand: A vendor can be 1099-eligible even before their W9 arrives, and having a W9 doesn't make someone 1099-eligible (corporations are exempt). Pushing `w9_on_file` as `Vendor1099` corrupts tax flags in both directions — it's wrong by design and a code review flag. The fix is straightforward: new boolean column, form field, and one-line change in the sync.
approach: Add `is_1099_eligible` boolean to `subcontractors` table (migration + fillable + default false)
Add the field to the Subcontractors CRUD form: a checkbox with a brief tooltip explaining 1099 vs W9
Update `VendorSync::sync()` — change `Vendor1099 => $sub->w9_on_file` to `Vendor1099 => $sub->is_1099_eligible`
Update the `VendorSync` unit test to assert the correct field is mapped — run the full suite to confirm green
resources: QB v3 Vendor entity docs: `Vendor1099` field definition
Subcontractors CRUD Vue component (Index + Edit/Create form)

tasks.title: Sandbox-validate all 6 entity QB mappings + upsert/LinkedTxn shapes
tasks.notes: BLOCKER before production. 6 sync() mappings written but UNTESTED against QB. Connect Intuit sandbox; validate field mappings, sparse upserts, Payment LinkedTxn shape, Estimate ItemRef requirement, Purchase TotalAmt output-only.

prep.what: Fire every sync service against a real Intuit sandbox and confirm the field mappings actually work — the fake-QB layer has covered flow but zero real API calls have been made.
prep.understand: This is the hard production blocker. The 6 sync services were written from QB API docs alone — the fake layer confirms the flow but masks real shape errors. Known landmines: `Payment.LinkedTxn` must be an array-of-objects (not a flat value); Estimate line items require `ItemRef` (the API will 400 without it); `Purchase.TotalAmt` is output-only and can't be set on create. Any one of these breaks silently against the real API. You need your `.env` Intuit sandbox credentials ready before this block.
approach: Set up an Intuit developer sandbox at developer.intuit.com — get Company ID, Client ID/Secret — run the OAuth flow locally to populate tokens in `quickbooks_connections`

Set `QB_FAKE=false` in `.env`. In `tinker`, create one of each entity (Client, Subcontractor, Estimate, Invoice, income Transaction, expense Transaction) and dispatch each sync job synchronously — inspect the QB sandbox dashboard
For each failure: read the Intuit error response (JSON `fault` array), trace to the sync service, fix the mapping, re-run
After all 6 pass: add mock-`DataService` unit tests per sync asserting the built object shape (especially LinkedTxn, ItemRef, TotalAmt)
resources: developer.intuit.com — sandbox company + OAuth2 app setup
QB v3 API Reference: Customer, Vendor, Estimate (ItemRef on lines), Invoice, Payment (LinkedTxn array shape), Purchase (TotalAmt output-only note)
`QB_FAKE` env flag — set to false before this session
